comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: Implemented, commit 40e42c962: receiver lifespan starts start_background_janitor (daemon, non-fatal try/except, stop event on shutdown). Lifespan test added (importorskip fastapi), passing locally with the reviewbot extra.

compiled_state.description:
## Reproduction Steps
1. src/rebar/_snapshot/janitor.py implements watermark/LRU GC (start_background_janitor, run_gc) but is referenced only from _snapshot/__init__.py and its unit tests — nothing in src/rebar/review_bot/ calls it.
2. Each review materializes content-addressed snapshots per origin/main SHA plus tickets-<sha> entries; the container /tmp is overlay → root disk.
3. Observed: 694M accumulated in 41h (container recreation is currently the only cleanup); ~400MB/day fills the 30G root disk in weeks — same failure mode as incident 2731 on a longer fuse.

**Expected:** snapshot store is bounded by the janitor. **Actual:** janitor never started; growth unbounded between deploys.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] start_background_janitor (or equivalent run_gc cadence) is invoked at review-bot startup with sane watermarks.
- [ ] Test asserting the app wiring starts the janitor.
- [ ] File paths: src/rebar/review_bot/app.py, src/rebar/_snapshot/janitor.py.
compiled_state.title: review-bot gate-snapshot janitor is dead code in production — /tmp/rebar-gate-snapshots grows unboundedly on the root disk
